hub_subtitle: Inglês para a vida real — falar, entender e ter autonomia no idioma

lesson.menu_title: How Long Have You...? -- Hobbies You've Had for Ages
lesson.menu_desc: Talking about how long you have been doing the things you love -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 6 -- How Long Have You...?
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ana Claudia Veraldi | Lesson 6 -- How Long Have You...?

listenings.text: People ask me how long I have been doing this, and I honestly have to stop and count. I have been restoring old furniture for about six years now, since we moved to the countryside. It started with one broken chair. I have been picking up the skills slowly, from videos and from my own mistakes, and I have been getting a little better every year. I am not fast. Some pieces have been sitting in my workshop for months, half finished, because I lose track of time and then I lose track of the project. But I have never once been bored. When you are really into something like this, the hours just disappear.

There is a guitar in the corner of my living room, and I have been playing it, badly, for nearly twenty years. I have never had a single lesson. I picked it up in my twenties because a friend was into it, and I have been teaching myself ever since. I still cannot read music. I have learned maybe forty songs, and I play the same ten over and over. My wife has been listening to those same ten songs for two decades, and she has never complained, which is either love or extraordinary patience. I have been meaning to take a real lesson for years. I probably never will.

extra_audio.text: First, Tom asks Ana how long she has been restoring furniture. Then Ana says she has been doing it since she moved to the countryside. Next, she explains that she has been teaching herself from videos. After that, Tom asks whether she has ever thought about selling her pieces. Finally, Ana says she has been thinking about it, but she is not ready yet.
